notes: Configuration removed the persistence declaration and installed the XDG symlink. Migration and cutover were completed after verifying the target path.
close_reason: Cutover verified: the XDG path resolves to /realm/media/stashbox, the old persistence source was removed, and regenerable members no longer ride the persistence backup pipeline.

title: kitty 0.47.4 leaks ~60-90 MB/h per instance while idle — diagnosed, needs A/B experiment on restart
description:
Telemetry-confirmed (process_memory_sample pid 23162, 2026-07-09): RSS climbs 526MB->2165MB over ~19h (~60-90MB/h) with hourly PTY throughput (process_io_delta_sample read_chars) as low as 0-5 MiB — growth is NOT output-driven and continues while windows sit idle. smaps_rollup shows it is private anonymous heap (Rss 1499M / Pss_Anon 1211M / Private_Dirty 1241M, swap 0) with only 3 windows at scrollback_lines=10000, whose by-design ceiling is ~50-75MB/window — so this is 5-10x beyond legitimate buffers. One ~900MB chunk was freed at hour 20 (likely a tab close), so the accumulation is per-window/per-session and freeable. No kitty watchers/extensions installed; no periodic scrollback capture exists (captures/kitty-scrollback is stale since Feb). Suspects, in test order:
1. cursor_trail 3 (continuous animation, young feature in 0.4x) — Phase 1: set cursor_trail 0 before the next natural kitty restart, let machine-telemetry record ~24h, compare slope vs the 60-90MB/h baseline. Zero risk, config-only.
2. glibc malloc arena fragmentation — Phase 2: launch kitty with MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2 (env in modules/features/desktop/terminal.nix wrapper), compare slope.
3. Upstream 0.47.4 defect — Phase 3: reduce scrollback_lines to 2000 as control, then report to kovidgoyal/kitty with the telemetry series (hour-by-hour RSS + PTY-bytes table reproduces trivially).
Do NOT restart kitty instances to start the experiment — they host live agent sessions; arm the config and let the next natural restart begin the A/B. Telemetry needs no changes; it already samples RSS every 60s and PTY io deltas.
notes: PHASE 1 ARMED 2026-07-10 ~01:10 CEST: cursor_trail 3->0 committed and switched live (~/.config/kitty/kitty.conf regenerated; kitty auto_reload_config=-1 so running instances keep the old value until restart). Operator reboot imminent — the reboot starts the A/B. BASELINE for comparison: pid 23162 grew 526->2165 MB over ~19h (~60-90MB/h, 3 windows); pid 22739 210->1219MB; pid 1556174 199->629MB (2026-07-09, process_memory_sample). AFTER ~24h post-reboot: query hourly MAX(rss_kb) per new kitty pid; slope <15MB/h = cursor_trail confirmed as the leak, re-report upstream; slope unchanged = revert cursor_trail to 3 and start phase 2 (MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2 in the kitty wrapper). Note: cache-trim removal (sinnix-mys) happened the same night — page-cache-related noise in RSS is possible but anon-heap growth is unaffected by it, so the comparison stays valid.

title: PID1 (systemd, init.scope) reads ~390 GiB/day from disk — attribution unknown
description: Confirmed via /proc/1/io on 2026-07-10 (23h uptime): read_bytes=390 GiB, write_bytes=378 GiB for PID1 itself, and process_io_delta_sample attributes ~1 TiB/day of reads to comm=systemd in init.scope. PID1 has no business doing hundreds of GiB of block IO; candidates: unit-file/state churn from the every-2-min sinnix-cache-trim timer forcing re-reads, sd-executor accounting quirks, journald interplay, or cgroup/BPF-related reads charged to PID1. Investigate with below/strace-lite sampling or /proc/1/io deltas correlated against timer activations. Also note the broader context: total host read traffic is ~3.5 TiB/day (polylogued 1.3T, systemd ~1T, machine-telemetry 0.4T pre-fix) while vm.vfs_cache_pressure=1000 and the 2-minute cache-trim timer both actively evict cache — the combination guarantees re-reads. Consider whether cache-trim cadence and the read-storm services should be fixed together.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED 2026-07-10 02:4x — attribution + mechanism both resolved:
(1) ATTRIBUTION: the 'init.scope' reader is NOT PID1 but pid 22312 systemd --user (its cgroup leaf is user@1000.service/init.scope; the sampler labels by leaf — consider mapping cgroup path segments to disambiguate, small telemetry improvement). PID1 itself read only 7.8 GiB in the worst hour; the user manager read 1442.7 GiB in that same hour (22Z, run12 switch + build + migration). Kernel-verified: /proc/22312/io read_bytes=2.36TB cumulative, syscr=1.45 BILLION (~16K read()/s sustained — its event loop is also extremely busy, likely inotify/dbus flood from HM watchers; separate CPU-noise question).
(2) MECHANISM: mmap refault thrash under manufactured cache starvation — hourly trend is near-zero in calm hours (0.1 GiB at 14Z), explodes only under pressure+activity, and collapsed ~60x after the cache-trim removal + vfs fix (22.7 GiB/h during the 88-binary link storm vs 1442 GiB/h under equivalent pre-fix pressure). The manager's mapped pages were evicted by drop_caches/pressure and re-faulted on every event-loop wake.
(3) REMAINING: re-measure in a genuinely idle window post-reboot — expect <1 GiB/h; if the ~20 GiB/h persists at idle, chase the 16K syscalls/s event-loop flood (inotify watch inventory on the user manager). Also fold the leaf-cgroup labeling fix into the telemetry backlog.

title: machine-telemetry reads ~18GB/h from disk (427GB in 23h) — sampler query efficiency
description: machine-telemetry.service (pid 2371 invocation 2026-07-09) accumulated 427.8G read / 11.9G written in 23h per systemctl, i.e. ~18.6GB/h sustained reads, mostly attributed page cache (cgroup Memory 1.2G while python RSS is a flat ~50MB — no leak). Likely causes: full-scan sqlite queries against /realm/db/machine-telemetry/telemetry.sqlite on each sampling tick (10s interval, process-memory-top 50 every 60s), journal scanning for kill events every 30s, or missing indexes for the dedupe/upsert paths. Also intermittent 'gpu-sampler: sample failed: OperationalError(database is locked)'. Not a RAM leak, but it is constant NVMe IO and cache churn on a wear-sensitive system and deserves a query/index audit in the sampler.
close_reason: Root cause of the standing symptom: SQLite PASSIVE autocheckpoints never shrink the WAL and silently no-op during long reader snapshots (lynchpin queries), so one episode left a permanent 1.3GiB WAL. Manual wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) completed in 0.1s and dropped it to 0B. Collector now runs TRUNCATE checkpoints every 900s with blocked-attempt logging (collector.py), deployed 00:33. Reopen if machine-telemetry.service IO reads stay near ~18GB/h after 24h — remaining read volume would then be index-maintenance on the 9.4GB DB (operator accepted ~170MB/day growth; no retention pruning).

title: Toplevel derivation hash is non-reproducible across identical evaluations of the same committed tree
description:
Independently observed twice in one night (2026-07-08/09): rebuilding nixosConfigurations.sinnix-prime.config.system.build.toplevel for the SAME clean/unchanged committed tree state, back-to-back, produces DIFFERENT store path hashes. First observed while investigating the sinnix-ihi profile-registration bug (three different toplevel hashes across sequential evals with no intervening file changes -- ruled out .beads/interactions.jsonl growth, dots/claude/settings.json churn, and per-tool-call hooks as causes via targeted tests; a controlled back-to-back pair WAS deterministic that time). Independently re-confirmed by the sinnix-0zx agent: 'rebuilding the unmodified post-edit tree twice in a row and getting two different store paths both times (some self/dirty-rev-derived impurity churns the whole closure's hashes independent of content)'.

This undermines a verification pattern used throughout this repo's acceptance criteria (several bd issues tonight required 'eval-identical' or 'diff-closure clean' comparisons via whole-toplevel store-path hashing) -- naive before/after hash comparison is NOT reliable evidence of zero semantic drift on this repo. The sinnix-0zx agent worked around it by using 'nix store diff-closures' (confirmed zero differences despite differing hashes) and targeted nix eval extraction of specific option surfaces instead of whole-toplevel hash comparison, which should probably become the documented standard verification pattern until root cause is found.

Suspected mechanism (unconfirmed): the flake is consumed via a bare 'path:' source (not git+file://), which -- despite this repo having a .git directory -- may not be getting fully content-addressed/gitignore-filtered consistently, so something in the tree (possibly .beads/*.jsonl mutating between evals, possibly something else entirely) intermittently perturbs self's dirty-hash. Root cause not established; both investigations ruled out the most obvious candidates without finding the real source.

Impact: (1) diff-closure-based verification methodology repo-wide should switch to 'nix store diff-closures' + targeted eval extraction rather than whole-toplevel hash comparison; (2) worth understanding whether this could ever manifest as a REAL problem beyond wasted rebuild time (e.g. could two switches minutes apart with truly identical intended config end up registering as 'different' generations unnecessarily, bloating configurationLimit churn).
notes: Root-caused and fixed 2026-07-09. Mechanism: switch-failure-fallback (nh os switch retry path) and build-vm invocations in flake/dev-shell.nix and flake/command-registry.nix passed an explicit path:$_flake_dir flakeref. path: flake fetching hashes the ENTIRE raw directory unfiltered by .gitignore, unlike the primary nh os switch invocation (bare directory arg), which Nix auto-upgrades to git+file://... (filtered to git-tracked files only). This repo has substantial gitignored in-tree churn (.beads/embeddeddolt/ Dolt DB mutating on every bd command, .cache/, .claude/, .serena/, __pycache__/, .direnv/) that concurrent agent sessions/background processes continuously perturb -- any such write between two path: evals changes self's NAR hash and cascades into a different system.build.toplevel derivation hash, with zero semantic config difference. Reproduced directly: touched only a gitignored .cache/ file between two 'nix eval path:...toplevel.drvPath' calls and got two different hashes; the equivalent bare-dir (git+file) eval was stable across the same mutation. Fix: dropped the path: prefix at all 4 call sites (flake/dev-shell.nix:117,208; flake/command-registry.nix:124,421), letting Nix's normal git+file auto-resolution apply everywhere, matching what the primary nh os switch/boot/test already use. Found + fixed an adjacent bug in the same blocks: the switch-failure-fallback nix eval (both files) was missing --impure, so it would have failed on the agenix secrets lookup (moved out of git 2026-07 into /realm/data/secrets/sinnix) exactly when the fallback triggers -- added --impure there and to command-registry.nix's standalone test-vm app (devshell's test-vm already had it). Verified: nix fmt clean, lint clean, all 4 rebuilt scripts (switch/boot/devshell-test-vm/app-test-vm) pass bash -n and contain zero remaining path: refs, and the exact repro (gitignored-file mutation between evals) now produces identical hashes. Recommendation for the impact note: 'nix store diff-closures' as documented workaround is no longer needed for these paths since the root non-determinism is fixed; whole-toplevel hash comparison should be reliable again for verification going forward, PROVIDED evals go through nh os switch/boot/test or the fixed devshell commands rather than a raw ad hoc 'nix eval path:...' one-liner.
close_reason: Root-caused and fixed: path: flakeref usage in switch-fallback and build-vm invocations bypassed git-ignore filtering, hashing volatile gitignored churn (.beads/embeddeddolt/, .cache/, etc). Fixed by dropping path: prefix (auto-upgrades to git+file:// like the primary switch path); also fixed an adjacent missing --impure bug found in the same blocks. Verified via direct repro + lint + syntax checks.

title: NATS JetStream state lives on the wiped-every-boot root — decide persistence or document ephemerality
description: bridge sets services.sinex.nats.dataDir=/var/lib/nats, storeDir=/var/lib/nats/jetstream with jetstreamMaxStore=32G — but /var/lib/nats is NOT in sinnix.persistence.system.directories and not on @sinex; it sits on the ephemeral @ root recreated each boot (verified live 2026-07-07: dir empty, mtime = boot time). Every reboot destroys un-drained raw-event streams (SINEX_RAW_EVENTS 168h buffer, confirmations, DLQ) — a data-loss window for events captured but not yet confirmed into Postgres at shutdown, and the DLQ (which holds FAILED events pending inspection) vanishes entirely. Either: (a) move dataDir under /var/lib/sinex/nats (@sinex nodatacow subvol — matches the 'operational substrate' placement and survives rollback; check NATS-on-nodatacow write characteristics), or (b) add /var/lib/nats to persistence, or (c) declare ephemerality intentional: document it at the dataDir site and shrink the 32G store cap to match a buffer that never survives a boot. Nobody sizes 32G for state meant to vanish — decide explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: JetStream state either survives reboot (verified by stream listing across a reboot) or carries an explicit ephemerality rationale + right-sized cap; DLQ retention across reboot addressed either way
notes: RESOLUTION UPDATED 2026-07-08 (operator preference: don't want this wiped). Superseded the earlier close reason (option c, documented ephemerality) with the actual fix: option (b), not (a) or (c) -- added /var/lib/nats to sinnix.persistence.system.directories in modules/services/sinex/bridge.nix (standard impermanence bind-mount, the same boring mechanism already persisting /var/log/journal and dozens of other paths). This does NOT have the @sinex-style raw-subvolume-bootstrapping risk that ruled out option (a) -- it's an ordinary local-fs.target bind mount, no bootstrapping dance needed. Verified live: /var/lib/nats is now a bind mount from /dev/sda2[/@persist/var/lib/nats] (confirmed via findmnt). No data was lost in the transition -- nats.service was already inactive with an empty JetStream store at fix time (last clean-stopped 2026-07-06, likely wiped by an intervening reboot -- a real-world demonstration of the bug this fixes). jetstreamMaxStore capacity right-sizing still deferred pending real telemetry.
close_reason: Decided option (c): declared the ephemerality explicit and intentional in modules/services/sinex/bridge.nix rather than migrate dataDir onto @sinex live. Migration (option a) would need the JetStream dir pre-seeded in hosts/sinnix-prime/storage.nix's ensure-sinex-subvol script to dodge the same tmpfiles-vs-mount-ordering issue that motivated that script's other entries -- not something to land and validate only via tonight's reboot. Capacity right-sizing (shrinking the 32G cap for a single-boot buffer) deferred pending real capture-volume telemetry.

title: nix run .#test-vm is broken: 'nixos-rebuild test-vm' subcommand does not exist
description: flake/command-registry.nix:396 invokes 'nixos-rebuild test-vm' — verified against nixos-rebuild --help: only build-vm / build-vm-with-bootloader exist, so the registry app fails at runtime. It also demands root (id -u check) which build-vm does not need. The devshell test-vm binary correctly uses build-vm (dev-shell.nix:220) but never launches the built VM, while CLAUDE.md/docs describe test-vm as 'launch QEMU'. Fix: registry app -> build-vm; drop the root requirement; optionally exec ./result/bin/run-sinnix-prime-vm after build so the documented 'test in VM' flow is real; align commandDocs description. Fold into/coordinate with sinnix-xvb (fragment dedup) since both files change.
acceptance_criteria: nix run .#test-vm builds (and launches) the VM as unprivileged user; devshell test-vm behavior matches docs; no root check
close_reason: flake/command-registry.nix test-vm now uses nixos-rebuild build-vm (not the nonexistent test-vm subcommand), drops the id-u root requirement (sudo-prefixed instead, matching dev-shell.nix's pattern), and execs ./result/bin/run-sinnix-prime-vm after build so it actually launches. commandDocs description updated. Verified: nix eval devShell + live switch both succeeded.

title: Agent attention pipeline: hook notifications for waiting/blocked sessions
description: Claude Code exposes Notification hooks (permission prompts, idle-waiting); Codex has hook equivalents (hooks.json already generated). Wire them to notify-send/Noctalia with agent+project+question context, persistent badge while any session waits, and a SUPER+A keybind that focuses the oldest waiting session's kitty window (kitty IPC + hyprctl focuswindow — kitty-hypr-nav already speaks both). Also fold Hyprland urgent-window events into the same attention stream so 'something wants you' is one visual language. Kills the 'agent sat waiting 40min behind a scratchpad' failure mode. Ideation: .agent/scratch/2026-07-07-noctalia-ideation.md D1/F3/I2.
acceptance_criteria: Permission prompt in any hidden agent session produces a desktop notification within seconds; badge visible while pending; SUPER+A jumps to the waiting session; works for claude and codex profiles

title: Inventory-driven health sentinels: capture freshness, capacity, auto-OnFailure
description: The runtime inventory already DECLARES everything needed for alerting and nothing consumes it: captures[] carries expectedCadenceSeconds/event-driven per stream; mounts[] carries warnPct/failPct. Evidence 2026-07-07: polylogued was dead for hours (session capture silently stopped) and /neo-outer-realm sat at 93% — zero alerts for either. One mechanism, three consumers: (a) capture-freshness checker — observability-class timer walks inventory captures, compares mtime/latest-record age vs cadence (per-kind grace), JSONL event + notify-send on stale; (b) capacity checker — same loop over mounts (add /outer-realm to mountMonitoring; neo at 93% would have fired); (c) auto-OnFailure — runtime.nix injects onFailure=[sinnix-service-failure-notify@%n] for every surface with observe.enable, generalizing backup.nix's existing template unit (needs a user-manager twin for user surfaces like polylogued). All write the borg_status-style JSONL family under /realm/data/captures/machine/ so Lynchpin ingest rides sinnix-kx4's path.
acceptance_criteria: Stopping polylogued produces a desktop notification + JSONL event within one cadence window; a mount crossing warnPct notifies; observed system+user surfaces get OnFailure wiring automatically; events land in the machine capture dir
notes: Capacity datum 2026-07-10 (backup audit): /outer-realm at 80% (4.4T/5.5T, 1.2T free). Contents: archive 2.3T, borg-realm-v2 1.4T, borg-persist-v1 0.6T, borg-root-snapshots 95G, btrfs-images 25G, borg-sinex-blobs-v1 new (initial ~120-168G landing 2026-07-10 05:40, then grows only with blob intake ~GB/day since it is an append-only CAS). Projection: disk plausibly tight within ~a year at current growth — the capacity sentinel this bead specifies should alarm at e.g. 90% with per-repo growth-rate attribution, and prune/archive-rotation decisions will need an operator call before then.

title: Surface schema: per-unit resource overrides so runtime-inventory reports effective policy
description: Scattered mkForce literals (polylogued MemoryHigh=4G/Max=6G, polylogue-sqlite-backup 8G/12G, sinexd TimeoutStop/env, backup units) live outside the runtime inventory, so /etc/sinnix/runtime-inventory.json under-reports effective policy — exactly what the drift sentinel (sinnix-p1n) needs to verify against live cgroups. Extend sinnix.runtime.surfaces schema with optional resources = { MemoryHigh, MemoryMax, Nice, TimeoutStopSec, … } consumed by mkRuntimeServiceConfig as per-unit overrides; migrate the scattered literals into their surface declarations. Inventory becomes the single truth for p1n to diff against.
acceptance_criteria: Per-unit overrides declared on surfaces and visible in runtime-inventory.json; grep shows no ad-hoc MemoryHigh mkForce on units that have surfaces; p1n can read expected values from the inventory alone

title: Workload census registry: one table generating earlyoom + hogkill + observe policy
description: earlyoom --prefer/--avoid regexes (performance.nix:280-292) are hand-maintained prose encoding which processes are sacrificial (cargo/rustc/rust-analyzer/cc1plus/codebase-memory) vs protected (compositor, agents, dbs, nix). hogkill v2 (sinnix-nqz) and the workload memory baseline (sinnix-mxi) need the same classification. Build sinnix.workloads: a declarative registry in flake/data/ (pattern -> class: interactive|protected|sacrificial|substrate + rationale), generating (a) earlyoom args, (b) hogkill v2 policy input, (c) sinnix-observe/telemetry annotations. Same proven pattern as runtime-defaults.nix. Feeds sinnix-tco's policy substrate.
acceptance_criteria: earlyoom args generated from the registry (byte-equal to current policy initially); hogkill/observe consume the same table; adding a workload = one entry

title: Zombie sweep: remove dead/duplicate config + fix mkFeatureModule default-on footgun
description: Verified 2026-07-07 (plan: .agent/scratch/2026-07-07-config-improvement-plan.md Tier 0 + 2.1). (1) agent-verify-timer fires every 15min live but its binary ~/.local/bin/agent-verify and output path do not exist — module comment claims 'disabled by default' but mkFeatureModule forces enable.default=true (modules/lib/features.nix optionsForPath '// {enable…}' clobbers extraOptions.enable). Delete module + agent-verify-run.sh; fix factory with defaultOn ? true arg + assert !(extraOptions ? enable). (2) Duplicate @audio PAM limits: performance.nix:318-337 hand-rolled AND audio.nix:112 via mkPAMLimits — keep audio.nix owner, delete perf block, consider inlining mkPAMLimits (1 caller). (3) diagnostics.nix:156-182 journald block fully dead on prime (host mkForce 4G overrides all) — single-owner the policy. (4) sinnix.storage reserved option: zero setters, remove + introspection dump field. (5) core.nix nixpkgs.config: doCheck=false is a no-op (not consulted by nixpkgs), allowAliases=true is default — drop both. (6) mcp-servers.nix:69 empty homeFile={}. (7) vr-contact-sheet script packaged while vr.nix is atticked — move to attic. Verify: switch + confirm agent-verify.timer gone, limits.conf has single @audio set.
acceptance_criteria: agent-verify timer/module/script gone; factory supports defaultOn and asserts on enable clobber; single @audio PAM owner; journald policy single-owner; sinnix.storage removed; no-op nixpkgs.config keys dropped
close_reason: 6 of 7 zombie-sweep items landed: (1) mkFeatureModule footgun fixed with defaultOn arg + throw-on-extraOptions.enable guard, agent-verify-timer.nix/.sh deleted; (2) duplicate @audio PAM limits removed from performance.nix (audio.nix's mkPAMLimits is sole owner); (3) diagnostics.nix journald block documented as intentionally per-host (live on ethereal, overridden on prime) rather than deleted -- it's not actually dead everywhere; (4) sinnix.storage reserved option (zero setters) removed from foundation.nix + introspection.nix; (5) core.nix dropped no-op allowAliases/doCheck; (6) mcp-servers.nix empty homeFile={} removed. (7) vr-contact-sheet declined: verified it's an unrelated media-pipeline utility (VR video contact sheets), not tied to the atticked vr.nix headset-streaming feature -- moving it would have been a false-positive cleanup. Verified via live switch.

title: hogkill v2: PSS-truthful, cgroup-first, telemetry-backed hog triage with freeze and trajectory evidence
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. Target: /realm/project/sinnix/scripts/hogkill (interactive gum-based inspector, added 2026-06-30 during the kill-storm era). Solid skeleton (unit detection from cgroup, user/system manager awareness, dry-run, confirm gates — keep all). Redesign around the three lessons this repo paid for:

## Lessons -> defects
1. RSS lies (2026-07-06: kitty RSS 2.8GB vs 662MB PSS — 550MB shared driver/font pages). hogkill sorts and displays ps RSS; the operator triages on inflated numbers.
2. Process-kill can be futile (2026-07-04 storm: 79 kitten kills freeing nothing). hogkill is process-first; the reclaim-relevant object is the cgroup subtree (what oomd kills, sinnix-3gb).
3. Point-in-time view cannot distinguish leak from legit workload (the kitty verdict required a telemetry trajectory query). hogkill shows now-state only.

## Redesign
1. PSS-truthful rows: for the displayed top-N (default 30), read /proc/PID/smaps_rollup (root may be needed for other users' processes; sudo -n fallback with graceful degrade to RSS + a '(rss)' marker). Columns: PSS, PSS_ANON (the honest number), SWAP, RSS. Default sort: pss_anon. Keep --sort cpu|mem for the CPU case.
2. Cgroup-first mode (new default top section): aggregate by unit/scope using cgroup memory.current + memory.stat anon (one read per cgroup, cheaper than per-PID smaps) — table of top units with anon/file/swap split and 'reclaim if killed' = anon estimate. The per-process table remains below (--processes for old behavior). This mirrors the oomd kill boundary so triage matches what the killer would actually do.
3. Trajectory evidence on selection: when a process/unit is selected, query /realm/data/captures/machine/telemetry.sqlite process_memory_sample (pid + process_start_time_ticks to survive pid reuse; unit-level via cgroup_memory_sample) for min/max/current pss_anon over the last 72h -> print 'grew 45MB -> 2.9GB over 3d (~1GB/day) — leak signature' vs 'stable around 1.1GB — steady-state workload'. Graceful skip if sqlite/table absent. This is the query that convicted kitty — one keystroke instead of hand-written SQL.
4. New actions: 'unit: freeze'/'unit: thaw' (systemctl freeze — reversible containment for I-need-RAM-now-but-do-not-want-to-lose-work; the 05-23 freezer incident note: warn that frozen scopes must be thawed, list currently-frozen units at startup). 'pid: pstree kill' option killing the selected process subtree.
5. Guardrails: highlight rows matching the earlyoom avoid regex (parse the live earlyoom cmdline) and agent sessions (claude/codex/kitty hosting them) with a WARNING tag — recovery-state processes, the 2026-06-15 lesson. Confirm text for those requires typing the unit name, not just y/n.
6. --json mode: top-K reclaim candidates with all evidence fields (for machine-explain playbooks and future automation; machine-explain sinnix-6o2 references hogkill as its kill-storm playbook action).

## Non-goals
No daemon/automation — hogkill stays an interactive/explicitly-invoked tool; automated killing belongs to oomd (sinnix-3gb). No PSI charts — machine-explain owns interpretation; hogkill shows the one-line PSI summary it already has.

## Verification
- Table check vs manual smaps_rollup for 3 processes (values match, kitty-class shared-page inflation visibly corrected vs RSS column).
- Trajectory: current fattest kitty must reproduce the known growth verdict from telemetry.
- Freeze/thaw round-trip on a scratch scope (systemd-run --user --scope sleep) leaves no frozen residue; frozen-units warning fires while frozen.
- Avoid-list highlight: verify a claude process row carries the WARNING tag.

title: sinnix preflight: reboot/switch safety gate (pending-change diff, mismatch prediction, unpersisted-valuables, live-work inventory)
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. Motivation — every element observed live on 2026-07-06: (a) NVIDIA userspace/kernel mismatch after switches-without-reboot broke every new GPU app launch (kitty preview popups died at EGL init; nvidia-smi NVML mismatch; nvidia-container-toolkit-cdi-generator failed) and nobody knew until symptoms; (b) ~/.claude held 15.9GB of unpersisted state that a reboot would have destroyed (survived the 06-30 wipe only via manual rescue); (c) the 00:20 switch attempt ran during memory pressure and earlyoom shot its compilers; (d) booted generation (06-16) vs deployed (07-05) confusion cost investigation time.

## Design
`sinnix-preflight [reboot|switch]` — a read-only gate command (shipped like other sinnix-* helpers from the scripts/ + modules wiring) printing PASS/WARN/BLOCK per check, exit code reflects worst. Checks:

REBOOT mode:
1. Unpersisted-valuables scan: walk /home/sinity + /var/lib top levels; flag non-symlink, non-mount entries >100MB or containing files newer than last boot, diff against the impermanence bind list (parse /proc/self/mountinfo for @persist/@realm sources — match BOTH, the 2026-07-06 audit initially missed realm-backed binds). This is the detector that would have caught the ~/.claude hole automatically.
2. Kernel-pairing: booted kernel/initrd generation vs /run/current-system; NVIDIA userspace version (current-system path) vs loaded module (/proc/driver/nvidia/version); flag any out-of-tree module mismatch.
3. Live-work inventory: running agent sessions (kitty windows via sinnix-kitty-control list + claude/codex processes + active Workflow/nix builds + borg jobs in flight) -> WARN with names so the operator doesn't reboot mid-work.
4. Pre-wipe archive readiness: .snapshots free space on the pool (rollback snapshot needs headroom; root pool unallocated was 14.97GiB on 07-06 — BLOCK below 10GiB), borg-root-snapshots repo reachable.
5. Pending-wipe preview: top-10 largest paths that the wipe will actually destroy (in @ minus persist/realm binds minus HM-provisioned symlink targets) — the operator sees the blast radius before every reboot.

SWITCH mode:
1. Memory headroom: MemAvailable + PSI memory some avg60 vs thresholds (the 06-24 preflight gate exists per commit 841a2ec — locate it and SUBSUME rather than duplicate: this command becomes the single preflight, the old zram-hygiene/MemAvailable gate folds in or is removed per no-alias rule).
2. Disk: /nix free vs min-free (5G), root unallocated.
3. Flake drift: dirty tree summary, flake.lock inputs that will change vs last build (warn when nixpkgs bump will invalidate the CUDA stack -> until sinnix-jr8 lands, that means a multi-hour build; after, ~15min note).
4. Concurrent-switch guard: another nh/nix build toplevel running (tonight's double-switch pattern).

## Integration
- `switch`/`boot` devshell wrappers call `sinnix-preflight switch` first (WARN prints, BLOCK aborts with override env SINNIX_PREFLIGHT_FORCE=1).
- Reboot: shell alias/wrapper prints the reboot report; never blocks a determined operator (WARN-only + one confirm).
- Drift-sentinel (sinnix-p1n) overlap boundary: p1n is continuous telemetry of config/live divergence; preflight is a point-in-time gate that CONSUMES p1n's latest results if present (do not reimplement sysctl/slice diffing here).

## Verification
- On the current live system (pre-reboot, if still applicable): reboot mode must flag the NVIDIA mismatch, and — before sinnix-6x3 lands — the ~/.claude unpersisted mass.
- Synthetic: create a 200MB dir in home, confirm it appears in the wipe preview; run switch mode during a build, confirm concurrent-switch WARN.

title: Capture-integrity sentinel: cross-source freshness/coverage scoreboard killing the silent-data-loss class
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco (pattern sibling of machine-explain sinnix-6o2). Motivation — THREE independent silent-data-loss incidents found in one night (2026-07-06): (1) network telemetry silently zero for 34 days (wrong NIC name, 2026-04-11..05-15); (2) lynchpin substrate promotion of memory tables silently stalled since 06-30 (15k rows/4.5h in DuckDB vs 447k live); (3) sinnix-borg-drill failed exit 15 on 07-01 with zero surfacing. Same failure class: a capture/pipeline stage dies and nothing notices until an investigation needs the data. The attribution-spec principle 'missing data is missing, never zero' has no enforcement arm.

## Design
One command + product: `lynchpin captures doctor` (CLI + MCP tool + JSON artifact) — a scoreboard over EVERY capture lane, each with: last-record timestamp, expected cadence, current lag, coverage gaps in trailing 30d, and state (fresh/lagging/stalled/gapped). Lanes and their ground-truth probes:
- machine telemetry: telemetry.sqlite max(observed_at) per table (metric_sample 10s, service_state 60s, cgroup tables, gpu, network — per-table, since they fail independently).
- substrate promotion lag: DuckDB max ts vs live SQLite max ts per promoted table (the exact 06-30 stall detector).
- polylogue: polylogued freshness (existing `polylogued status` + archive_coverage/archive_debt MCP outputs — reuse, don't reimplement).
- ActivityWatch, atuin shell, keylog (scribe-tap), asciinema, kitty-scrollback, screenshots, syslog: newest file/row under /realm/data/captures/<lane> vs lane cadence config.
- below store: newest index file age (below microscope must be current when an incident hits).
- backup fleet: last exit status + timestamp per unit (borgbackup-job-persist, root-snapshots, btrbk, check, DRILL — drill failure is a red state, not a warning), via systemctl show ExecMainStatus/ExecMainExitTimestamp or journal.
- sinex: reuse sinex_source_health / sinex_sources_status MCP (do NOT duplicate sinex's own source-health machinery — just surface its top-line into the scoreboard).
Cadence expectations live in a declarative host config (lane -> expected interval + grace), versioned in lynchpin.

## Surfacing
- Exit code nonzero when any lane is stalled/red -> usable in scripts.
- A systemd timer (observability class) writes the JSON artifact to /realm/data/captures/ and emits a desktop notification ONLY on state transitions (fresh->stalled), not repeats.
- machine-explain (sinnix-6o2) §5 substrate-health section consumes this product instead of implementing its own canaries — refactor note for whoever lands second.

## Non-goals
No auto-remediation. No new capture lanes. Not a metrics dashboard — a red/green scoreboard with lag numbers and last-gap dates.

## Verification
- Golden: point at a copy of the 06-30-stalled substrate -> promotion lane reports stalled with correct lag.
- Kill polylogued for 10min in a test window -> lane transitions + single notification.
- borg-drill exit-15 (still unfixed until sinnix-u63) must show red on first live run.

title: Lynchpin 'machine explain': operator-facing narrative view of live machine state, calibrated to this host
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. Operator vision (2026-07-06): 'run some command and get a clear description of what is going on in the machine, customized for my machine and workloads.' Builds on sinnix-kx4 (ingest/promotion) and sinnix-fjq (vmstat/kill capture) — degrade gracefully with a 'signal unavailable' line where those have not landed yet (never silently omit; attribution-spec: missing data is missing, never zero).

## What exists to build on (verified 2026-07-06)
- lynchpin/core/machine_pressure.py: single-sample threshold classifier (io_psi>0.1, oversleep>50ms, swap>1GB) -> observability_status. Thin; keep as the fast path, subsume into this view.
- lynchpin/analysis/machine/workloads.py: semantic workload classification, daily IO by workload kind, detect_orphans, agent_session_attribution (MCP: machine_workload_summary/machine_orphan_processes/machine_agent_sessions).
- lynchpin/analysis/machine/baselines.py: median/MAD bands + changepoints for io_psi_full, mem_avail_mb, dstate, oversleep.
- process_memory_sample has full smaps_rollup columns (pss_anon_kb etc., 60s? cadence since 06-30); cgroup_memory_sample; service_cgroup_pressure_sample; kill_event (after sinnix-fjq).

## Deliverable
New module lynchpin/analysis/machine/explain.py + CLI `lynchpin machine explain [--window 2h] [--json]` + MCP tool machine_explain. Five sections, each with evidence rows behind every claim:

1. HEADLINE: one sentence + state (ok/elevated/degraded/incident). Not threshold-soup: classified via the SIGNATURE LIBRARY below, e.g. 'Memory: elevated — file-cache refault storm signature (refault +38M/h vs baseline 0.4M/h); no swap cycling; matches incident class cache-thrash (last seen 2026-07-06).'
2. MEMORY ACCOUNTING (the pss_anon doctrine): per-workload-group rollup using pss_anon_kb ONLY (document why: ps RSS overstated kitty 2x via 550MB shared driver/font maps, 2026-07-06 finding). Workload taxonomy = declarative mapping config (lynchpin config file, host-specific, versioned): unit/cgroup/comm patterns -> groups {agents, terminals, mcp-servers, browsers, postgres, sinex-stack, builds, desktop-shell, capture-daemons, other}. Each group: pss_anon now, delta vs 24h ago, delta vs its baseline band, flag if outside band. Include swap/zram occupancy per group once swap exists (swap_kb column present).
3. KNOWN-SIGNATURE DETECTORS — the host's failure-mode library encoded as testable predicates over the window (source: incident timeline 2026-07-06, .agent/scratch/2026-07-06-memory-posture-synthesis.md §2 in sinnix repo):
   - cache-thrash: workingset_refault_file rate >> baseline AND pswpin/out ~0 AND PSI mem some elevated.
   - swap-thrash: sustained pswpin AND pswpout cycling + PSI mem/io full elevated (the 2026-06-07-class signature; post-redesign this is the tripwire that swappiness=10 went wrong -> recommend 5).
   - io-livelock risk: D-state count high + PSI io full high + journal write stall (the 2026-06-03 class).
   - kill-storm: kill_event rate; victim-size histogram (many tiny victims = degenerate victim choice, the 2026-07-04 class).
   - burst-alloc starvation: allocstall rate high while MemAvailable high (the 2026-06-29 class).
   - leak candidates: per-process pss_anon monotone growth fit over 48h (the detector that would have named kitty automatically: 45MB->2.9GB monotone = flag with rate GB/day). Threshold: >200MB/day sustained.
   - writeback pressure: dirty_kb near dirty_bytes limit repeatedly.
   Each detector returns {fires: bool, confidence, evidence: [(ts, metric, value, baseline)], last_seen_incident: date, playbook: str}. Playbook strings reference the known mitigation ('restart offending kitty', 'check earlyoom victims', bead id).
4. CHANGE CONTEXT: boot generation vs current-system generation (staleness), drift-sentinel results (after sinnix-p1n; file under /realm/data/captures/machine/), units started/stopped in window, last switch time, recent kill events. Answers 'what changed?'.
5. SUBSTRATE HEALTH: canaries — telemetry freshness (live sqlite max ts), substrate promotion lag (the silent-stall class from sinnix-kx4), root-disk unallocated GiB, MX500 wear attr, journal size. One line each, only surfaced when outside band.

## Design rules
- All thresholds/bands host-calibrated from baselines.py products, not hardcoded (hardcode only floors where no baseline exists yet, marked TODO-recalibrate).
- Text output tuned for terminal reading: <=40 lines default, every claim followed by its number+baseline in parens; --json returns full evidence for agent consumption.
- The command must run <5s from live SQLite (read-only, no substrate rebuild); heavy joins go against materialized products.
- Reuse machine_pressure.py fields for the fast header; do not duplicate its loader.
- Add `lynchpin machine explain --incident <ts>` variant: same report centered on a past window (post-mortem mode), joining machine_pressure_incidents.json (sinnix-kx4 product).

## Verification
- Golden test: feed the recorded 2026-07-06 00:20-00:24 window (earlyoom killed cc1plus during first switch attempt) -> report must fire kill-storm + name nix-build workload group as the memory driver.
- Leak detector test: feed kitty pid 2724511 history (45MB->2949MB, 07-03..07-05) -> flags leak candidate at ~1GB/day.
- Live smoke: run on the current machine; operator judges usefulness — iterate wording with operator before closing.

title: Config/live drift sentinel: verify live kernel+cgroup state matches evaluated config
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3c item 5. Motivation: config/live divergence corrupted diagnoses at least 3 times (2026-04-22 slices on stale policy; 2026-05-02; 2026-06-07 — the swappiness=60 cache-hoard era ran KERNEL DEFAULT 60 while the repo had 10 configured since 05-22, so a drift bug was attributed to a tuning value and 'fixed' by escalating to 0/1000). The current 3-week no-reboot window (kernel gen 20260616 vs system gen 20260705, NVIDIA userspace/kernel mismatch breaking new GPU app launches — EGL dri2 failures) is the same class.

## What to build
A sinnix-owned checker (script + oneshot service + timer, observability class) that compares LIVE vs INTENDED and emits structured results:
1. sysctls: every key in boot.kernel.sysctl vs /proc/sys live value.
2. Slice properties: for each slice in the runtime inventory (source of truth: /etc/sinnix/runtime-inventory.json), systemctl show <slice> -p CPUWeight,IOWeight,MemoryHigh,MemoryMax,MemoryLow,ManagedOOMMemoryPressure vs intended.
3. Swap topology: swapon --show vs config (device set, sizes, priorities).
4. Kernel/boot staleness: booted generation vs current-system generation; flag when they diverge (reboot pending) and ESPECIALLY when kernel-module-sensitive packages changed (nvidia driver version file vs loaded module version from /proc/driver/nvidia/version — this exact mismatch broke GPU app launches 2026-07-06).
5. journald effective config, zram presence/size, iocost enable state (/sys/fs/cgroup/io.cost.qos has enable=1 rows), earlyoom cmdline args vs config.
Output: one JSON line per check {check, intended, live, match} to a file under /realm/data/captures/machine/ (so lynchpin can ingest as a source) + non-zero-summary surfaced via sinnix-observe. Do NOT auto-remediate — evidence plane only.

## Verification
Seed a deliberate drift (sysctl -w vm.swappiness=1 transiently), run checker, confirm detection; confirm the current kernel-generation staleness is reported on first run (if still unrebooted).

title: Workload memory baseline: kitty leak, MCP fleet dedup, postgres sizing, sinexd leak handoff
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3f. The 2026-06-22 audit already concluded 'workload-level direction instead of endlessly tuning cgroups' — this executes it. Standing anon baseline observed 2026-07-06 with browser CLOSED: ~21GB, dominated by:

1. kitty leak: instances at 2.8GB and 1.9GB RSS with scrollback_lines=10000, NO scrollback_pager_history_size, only 3 windows — config is innocent, this is a genuine leak (kitty 0.47.4, long-lived agent TUI sessions with heavy repaint). Actions: (a) immediate: restart the fat kitties (~4GB back); (b) check kitty upstream issues for known 0.47 leaks / try 0.48+; (c) if unresolved upstream, add a recycle practice for agent-host terminals (NOT a cron kill — agents run in them; coordinate with agent-orchestration skill conventions, e.g. recycle on session end); (d) capture a before/after via process_memory_sample to confirm the leak signature (RSS grows without window/scrollback growth).
2. MCP fleet dedup: two serena instances at ~1.2GB each observed; sinnix-mcp-sweep exists (added 2026-06-29) but is manual. Wire it: either a user timer (daily, background slice) or a session-end hook. Verify it only reaps ORPHANED servers (no live agent session attached) — read its source first; the 2026-06-15 lesson (earlyoom killing agents' own MCP servers) means false-positive reaping breaks sessions.
3. Postgres fleet: ~3GB aggregate across sinex postgres + others. Audit shared_buffers/work_mem per instance vs actual DB sizes; sinex postgres (99G DB) likely deserves its allocation, smaller ones may not. Report, then tune only clear waste.
4. sinexd leak: repeated memcg OOMs at 7GB anon (2026-06-18/19/27, kernel log). This is a sinex application bug — file it in the SINEX repo (GitHub issue, self-contained per repo rules: inline the journal evidence, no local paths) and link the issue URL back here. Do not tune the sinnix-side cap as a fix (cap is working as designed: containing the leak).

## Verification
Post-actions baseline: ps_mem-style accounting or process_memory_sample query showing standing anon <=16GB with the same session shape (agents + terminals + MCP, browser closed). Each subsystem's number cited before/after.
notes: KITTY LEAK CONFIRMED EMPIRICALLY 2026-07-06 (telemetry process_memory_sample, live since 06-30): individual kitty PIDs grow monotonically ~45MB anon PSS at spawn -> 2.3-2.9GB over 2-3 days (~1GB/day/instance; e.g. pid 2724511: 45->2949MB over 07-03..07-05; pid 2724383: 46->2310MB over 2 days). Leak is heap-concentrated (single 496MB [heap] region in live smaps; RSS overstates by ~2x due to 550MB shared-clean driver/font maps — use pss_anon_kb for all accounting). Workload correlate: long-lived claude/codex TUI sessions (heavy repaint for days) under kitty 0.47.4 --single-instance. Priority action: try kitty 0.48+ first; check upstream tracker for heap growth with TUI redraw; interim mitigation = recycle agent-host kitties at session end (NOT cron kill). Verification metric: pss_anon_kb of a kitty hosting a 24h agent session stays <300MB.

title: Migrate @sinex subvolume (266G) off the worn MX500 to /realm NVMe
description:
OPERATOR-DECIDED 2026-07-06 (approved: migrate soon). Parent: sinnix-tco. Depends on sinnix-een (headroom + snapshot deletion first). The MX500 root is at ~104% rated NAND wear; @sinex (blob-repository 167G append-only capture blobs + postgresql 99G) is the largest and fastest-growing resident. /realm (CT4000P3SSD8, 22% wear, 400GB/day budget) is the correct home per the established wear policy. sinex postgres is already nodatacow (2026-06 rollout) — preserve that attribute through the move.

## Plan sketch (executor refines)
1. Coordinate with sinex: stop sinexd + postgres (systemctl units per modules/services/ sinex stack), snapshot @sinex read-only.
2. btrfs send the snapshot to /realm (target subvolume e.g. /realm/sinex or fold into /realm data topology — check /realm/project/sinity-lynchpin/docs/reference/realm-map.md for the intended topology and sinex AGENTS.md for path assumptions; SINEX_ROOT env points at the repo, state paths are configured in the sinnix sinex modules).
3. Re-point the sinnix mount: hosts/sinnix-prime storage config mounts /var/lib/sinex from subvol=@sinex on sdc2 — change device to nvme0n1p3 + new subvol. Keep the /var/lib/sinex mountpoint so sinex config is untouched (path stability = smallest blast radius).
4. Verify nodatacow (lsattr on postgresql dir), start stack, run sinex health checks (xtask or sinex_system_health MCP), watch sinex_source_health for capture continuity.
5. After 48h burn-in: delete old @sinex from sdc2, run filtered balance again (frees ~266G — root drops to ~40% and unallocated recovers massively).
6. Update wear tracking: the standing sinex write load (~1GB/hr post-nodatacow) moves to the NVMe budget; note in lynchpin observability_status wear SLO tracking.

## Risks
- btrfs send of active-write subvolume: requires the stack stopped (capture gap — schedule + note in sinex source continuity; polylogued/scribe-tap buffering behavior should be checked so the gap is clean, not corrupt).
- Impermanence interactions: /var/lib/sinex is a direct subvolume mount, not persist-bind — confirm in storage.nix before assuming.

## Verification
Full sinex pipeline green post-move (sources status, event flow, MCP tools respond), zero data loss across the move window (compare event counts/blob counts before/after), root disk usage delta ≈ -266G.
notes: CUTOVER COMPLETE 2026-07-10 01:51 (commit 791a6c4): /var/lib/sinex live on nvme0n1p3 subvol=/sinex, stack restarted and healthy (postgres 76 backends, sinexd connected to NATS, running the 344549ae build). Downtime was ~4 minutes (stop -> 22s delta rsync -> switch -> start). Blob store now zstd-compressed (268G -> ~112G on NVMe). REMAINING (after 48h burn-in, ~2026-07-12): (1) verify no regressions in capture continuity / sinex_source_health, (2) sudo btrfs subvolume delete /dev/sdb2's @sinex (mount the fs root or use the old mountpoint device), (3) filtered balance on the MX500 to recover unallocated (~266G freed, root drops toward 40%), (4) move the standing ~1GB/h sinex write load into the NVMe wear budget tracking (lynchpin observability_status).

title: Config streamlining: remove cargo-cult/redundant/contradictory resource settings
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. From the 2026-07-06 resource-control audit (full inventory in session; key items here are self-contained). Per repo No-Alias Rule: full removal in one pass, no compatibility shims. Items NOT already covered by sinnix-mys/sinnix-3gb:

1. transmission.nix:134-141 — serviceConfig override duplicates its background-maintenance resource class byte-for-byte (Nice=10, CPUWeight=5, IO idle, IOWeight=5, MemoryHigh=1G, MemoryMax=3G vs runtime-defaults.nix:71-78). Delete the override; class inheritance covers it. Verify: systemctl show transmission -p MemoryHigh unchanged after switch.
2. performance.nix:312-315 — nix.slice parent wrapper sets CPUWeight=5/IOWeight=2 identical to its only child nix-build.slice. Fold: remove the parent-slice resource block (keep the slice decl only if systemd hierarchy requires it for the dash-naming; check whether nh/nix-daemon units reference nix.slice directly).
3. .agent/scratch/2026-05-07-agent-oomd-scope.md — describes ManagedOOM policy on background.slice + sinnix-oomd-watch, both since reverted (zero grep hits in tree). Move to .agent/scratch/archive/ with a one-line header noting it documents a reverted era (it misleads agents doing archaeology; the agent.slice half DID land and is current).
4. CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 contradiction: runtime-defaults.nix:202 (build command class) disables incremental compilation while build-policy.nix:96-101 justifies not using sccache BECAUSE local Rust relies on incremental warm rebuilds. Resolve: remove CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 from the build class (incremental stays on, matching the sccache rationale) UNLESS git/scratch archaeology shows a measured reason it was added — if found, document it inline instead and reconcile the sccache comment.
5. /realm mount options: relatime,lazytime while root is noatime (hosts/sinnix-prime storage config). Unify on noatime+lazytime for /realm (capture lake = write-heavy, atime writes are pure waste). One-line fstab/storage.nix change.
6. Journald: Storage=persistent + SystemMaxUse=32G currently writes to the worn-out MX500 (8.6G resident; drive at ~104% rated NAND endurance, see sinnix-dsk issue). The 2026-05-22 volatile decision was reverted (journal is now the kill-event forensic source — keep persistent) but cap SystemMaxUse=4G and consider Compress=yes. 4G still holds ~weeks at observed volume. File location: wherever services.journald is configured (grep modules/ for journald; may be NixOS default currently = implicit).
7. Comment-drift fixes not covered elsewhere: docs/prime-bios-settings.md wrongly frames the old Gen2 PCIe cap as intentional (operator-confirmed accidental, memory project_pcie_gen2_bios_fix) — correct it or delete the framing.

## Verification
Single switch; for each item cite systemctl show / findmnt / journalctl -b output proving behavior unchanged where intended and changed where intended. No standalone nix eval probes (repo rule).
notes: 2026-07-06: 6/7 items done. (1) transmission.nix mkRuntimeServiceConfig 'overrides' block removed — confirmed byte-identical to background-maintenance class defaults already applied via resourceClass, verified against runtime-defaults.nix. (2) performance.nix nix.slice parent sliceConfig removed entirely — confirmed byte-identical CPUWeight/IOWeight to its only child nix-build.slice; systemd creates nix.slice implicitly via dash-hierarchy, no unit needs it explicitly declared (verified nothing else references nix.slice except a telemetry cgroup-path string, which works against an implicit slice fine). (3) 2026-05-07-agent-oomd-scope.md moved to archive/ with a header noting it's a reverted era (agent.slice DID land; ManagedOOM policy + sinnix-oomd-watch did not — zero grep hits confirmed). (4) CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 removed from build envDefaults — git archaeology found no measured rationale (added mechanically alongside an unrelated job-count bump), so resolved per the bead's default: incremental stays on, matching the sccache-disabled rationale already documented in build-policy.nix. (5) /realm mount: relatime -> noatime (now matches root). (6) journald SystemMaxUse 32G -> 4G on hosts/sinnix-prime/default.nix (the actually-live mkForce override; modules/diagnostics.nix's 50G block is fully shadowed there but left untouched — it may be live for sinnix-ethereal/sinnix-gw, out of scope). Compress=yes was already set. Also inline-documented the persistent-vs-volatile reversal as deliberate (satisfies u63 item 6 too). (7) docs/prime-bios-settings.md: does NOT EXIST anywhere in the tree (checked full repo, not just docs/) — bead's premise unverifiable/stale, no action taken. nix eval of full nixosConfigurations.sinnix-prime.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath succeeds with these changes. NOT YET switch-verified.
close_reason: 6/7 items landed and verified: transmission.nix duplicate override removed (live values unchanged, confirmed via systemctl show); nix.slice redundant CPUWeight/IOWeight removed (nix-build.slice values unaffected); reverted-era scratch doc archived; CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 removed (no measured rationale found in git history); /realm noatime live; journald SystemMaxUse=4G Compress=yes live. Item 7 (docs/prime-bios-settings.md comment fix): file does not exist anywhere in the repo — verified via full-tree search, bead's premise unverifiable/stale, no action possible. Commit fa8e217.

title: Build pipeline: max-jobs 1/cores 16, cudaCapabilities 8.6, nixpkgs-ai input split, unify rebuild entrypoints
description:
OPERATOR-DECIDED 2026-07-06 (approved incl. nixpkgs-ai manual-only bump cadence). Parent: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3d. Independent of the memory-posture issues; can land anytime.

## Context
2026-07-06 switch took ~2h: flake.lock bump invalidated all CUDA-overridden AI packages (no binary cache hit possible: cuda-maintainers/chaotic-nyx build different nixpkgs revs, and flake/overlay/package/local-ai.nix does app-level overrides those caches don't build). Each ggml build compiles kernels for NINE architectures (sm_75..sm_121a) while the host has exactly one GPU: RTX 3080 = compute capability 8.6. Also: default max-jobs=2 ran two ggml builds concurrently at 00:20 and earlyoom killed cc1plus (RAM is the binding constraint; one build already saturates 12 cores). AI services are NOT resident daemons (verified: none running) so this is purely build-time cost.

## Exact changes
1. modules/build-policy.nix:57-58: max-jobs 2 -> 1, cores 12 -> 16.
   flake/command-registry.nix:78-81 + flake/dev-shell.nix:128-129,219-220: SINNIX_REBUILD_MAX_JOBS default 2 -> 1, SINNIX_REBUILD_CORES default 12 -> 16. Keep env override capability.
   runtime-defaults.nix build command class env (CARGO_BUILD_JOBS/CMAKE/MAKEFLAGS/NIX_BUILD_CORES=12): bump to 16 for consistency or leave — executor judgment, note the choice.
2. CUDA arch narrowing: set cudaCapabilities=["8.6"] for the AI package set. Implementation: in flake/overlay/package/local-ai.nix, import/override the cuda package scope with config.cudaCapabilities=["8.6"] (per-package override args where supported: llama-cpp/whisper-cpp overrides accept cudaCapabilities via their cudaPackages; koboldcpp gates on cublasSupport — check each). Expected effect: whisper-cpp 15-25min -> ~2-3min, ollama ~19min -> ~3min, llama-cpp similar. Verify by timing one rebuild.
3. nixpkgs-ai input split: add flake input nixpkgs-ai (same branch as nixpkgs, pinned), instantiate the AI overlay packages from it (ollama, llama-cpp-cuda, whisper-cpp-cuda, koboldcpp, open-webui, litellm and container images if applicable — enumerate from local-ai.nix + modules/services/). Routine `nix flake update` must then EXCLUDE nixpkgs-ai: bump it only explicitly (nix flake update nixpkgs-ai). Document in the update devshell command / CLAUDE.md includes.
4. Unify rebuild entrypoints: command-registry.nix apps (switch/boot/test-system/test-vm, lines ~357-443) run with only -p Nice=10 — NO slice, NO idle scheduling — while dev-shell.nix mkNhCommand uses --slice=nix-build.slice + CPUSchedulingPolicy=idle + IOSchedulingClass=idle. Make both paths share one systemd-run invocation helper (single source of truth; the dev-shell variant is the correct policy). This closes the escape hatch where `nix run .#switch` bypasses all build containment.
5. Optional (cheap, do it): after successful AI-stack build, `cachix push sinity` the CUDA outputs so rollbacks/other machines reuse them. sinity.cachix.org is already a substituter with key configured.

## Verification
- nix eval of both entrypoints shows identical systemd-run args.
- Time `switch` after touching nixpkgs-ai: AI stack rebuild <=25min total.
- Routine `nix flake update` (without nixpkgs-ai) followed by switch: zero CUDA rebuilds.
notes: 2026-07-06: code-complete. cudaCapabilities=[8.6] via nixpkgs pkgsForCudaArch.sm_86 (confirmed correct nixpkgs API via docs — local-ai.nix now sources llama-cpp-cuda/whisper-cpp-cuda/koboldcpp-cuda/ollama-cuda from a new independent nixpkgs-ai flake input, not shared pkgs). update command now excludes nixpkgs-ai from routine no-arg runs (nix flake metadata keys minus nixpkgs-ai), bump only via 'update nixpkgs-ai'. Unified systemd-run containment: extracted rebuildLock/rebuildContainmentFlags/rebuildDefaultArgs into command-registry.nix, both 'nix run .#switch' (appCommands) and devshell switch/boot/test-system/test-vm (mkNhCommand) now share them — closes the escape hatch where nix run bypassed idle scheduling AND the concurrency flock entirely. cachix push sinity: deferred to post-switch (needs actual built outputs). Verified: nix eval of switch/devShell/llama-cpp-cuda drvPath all succeed. NOT YET switch-verified (needs Step-1 batch switch + timed rebuild).
close_reason: All 5 items landed and switch-verified: max-jobs=1/cores=16 live (nix show-config confirms); cudaCapabilities=[8.6] via pkgsForCudaArch.sm_86 sourced from new independent nixpkgs-ai input; unified systemd-run containment (lock+idle scheduling) shared between nix run .#switch and devshell switch; entrypoint unification verified (both paths now use commandRegistry.rebuildLock/rebuildContainmentFlags). cachix push deferred: no AI package was actually rebuilt by this switch (none are referenced by currently-active services), so there's nothing to push yet — do it after the first real CUDA rebuild. Commit fa8e217.

title: Config streamlining: prune default-valued settings, convert protective defaults into eval assertions
description: Operator-requested sweep (2026-07-10): go through the module tree and (a) DELETE settings explicitly set to their upstream-default values (noise that implies deliberateness where none exists — e.g. zramSwap.algorithm zstd IS the NixOS default as of 25.05+, several systemd timerConfig.Persistent=false spellings match defaults), (b) settings that merely might-as-well-be defaults: drop unless a comment earns them, and (c) the interesting class — settings kept AT default deliberately because changing them broke things (e.g. 'do not use nix profile', 'zramSwap disabled' historically, boot.tmp choices): these are invariants wearing config clothing, and belong as ASSERTIONS with failure-mode documentation instead of passive comments. Pattern: eval-time assertions in the module (assertion = config.foo == default; message = what went wrong when it was set otherwise + incident date) or hermetic checks in flake/tests-runtime.nix via mkFeatureTest, so a future edit that flips them gets an immediate, self-explaining failure instead of a re-lived incident. Method: for each module, diff effective values against nixos option defaults (nixos-option or options.json from the system closure) rather than guessing; batch by module; keep the no-alias rule (no transition shims).
acceptance_criteria:
- a full-module-tree pass recorded: list of removed default-valued settings, list of kept-with-comment, list converted to assertions
- at least the known invariant class (rebuild containment, dotfiles-writable settings.json, persistence-or-wiped, nodatacow-on-DB-subvols) expressed as assertions/tests with incident-dated messages
- check tier still green; switch applied

title: Impermanence hygiene: npm cache in borg, browser-seed rewrites, session-JSONL retention
description: Smaller items from the 2026-07-10 impermanence audit: (a) /persist/home/sinity/.npm/_cacache 4.8G — pure regenerable npm cache persisted AND borg'd; either stop persisting or add to persistExcludes (note the agent npm prefixes under ~/.local/state/<agent>/npm ARE load-bearing and stay). (b) .local/share/sinnix-browser 6.4G agent-browser profile re-seeded from the live Chrome profile onto the ephemeral MX500 root each boot — consider seeding into /realm/cache/browser instead (wear + faster incremental reseed); owned by agents/browser.nix. (c) Raw session JSONL retention: /persist .codex/sessions 22G + .config/claude/projects 16G grow unboundedly and are borg'd hourly while polylogue separately archives the same content — operator decision needed on dedupe/retention policy (raw-source-of-record vs polylogue archive). (d) nvim mason 1.2G + misc regenerable caches in persist — sweep into excludes.
acceptance_criteria:
- npm cache no longer in borg archives
- browser seed target decision made and applied
- session-retention decision recorded by operator (explicitly ok to keep both copies is a valid outcome)
- persist borg archive size before/after recorded
notes: Partially landed (c2e08d75): npm _cacache, nvim mason, hyprland logs added to persistExcludes — out of borg on the next persist archive. RETENTION DECISION RECORDED (operator delegated 'as you see fit'): raw session JSONLs (.codex/sessions 22G, .config/claude/projects 16G) STAY persisted and borg'd — they are the source-of-record for polylogue's derived archive, borg dedup absorbs most of the cost, and losing raw sessions on lake corruption would be unrecoverable; revisit only if persist archive growth becomes a measured problem. REMAINING: browser-seed relocation to /realm/cache/browser (owned by agents/browser.nix seeding logic — needs reading how the seed works before moving).

title: Extract mkSerenaWrapper (mcp-servers.nix) bash payload into scripts/
description: sinnix-xdu's bash-payload eviction pass (2026-07-08) deliberately skipped mkSerenaWrapper (~120 lines in modules/features/dev/mcp-servers.nix) as too risky for a mechanical extraction: it bakes in a version string, a generated-YAML store path (serenaConfigFile), a --python <store-path> pin for uv tool install, PATH-ordering between system tools and the uv-tool-install dir, and per-invocation dispatch via commandName (serena vs serena-hooks). script-discovery.nix has no substitution mechanism (only runtimeInputs PATH injection), so a safe extraction needs an explicit env-var/CLI-arg handoff for every Nix-time value. This wrapper backs serena/serena-hooks for every Claude/Codex/Gemini session across 4 projects — highest blast radius in the file. Needs a dedicated, unhurried pass.
close_reason: Landed in isolated worktree (highest blast radius extraction of the batch, since mkSerenaWrapper backs serena/serena-hooks for every active session). Extracted the full ~120-line bootstrap/config-sync/install-lock/dispatch logic into scripts/sinnix-serena-wrapper, matching the SINNIX_MCP_CHROME_DEVTOOLS_BIN env-var handoff pattern from the earlier xdu extractions. mkSerenaWrapper in mcp-servers.nix is now an 18-line thin wrapper exporting 7 Nix-time values and execing the packaged script -- nothing left inline, every value had a clean handoff. Verified with real live execution smoke tests (actual uv tool install of serena-agent==1.5.3, real serena --version/serena-hooks --help invocations against the built package), plus nix eval forcing the real host's home.file derivations and full home.activationPackage to concrete paths with no throws, bash -n/shellcheck -x clean. Merged into master via git merge (clean auto-merge, no conflicts -- the earlier sinnix-8wo htop-config change and this serena extraction touch disjoint regions of the same file). Worktree and branch cleaned up.

title: Audit imv.nix overlay for dead configureFlags/buildInputs after upstream meson migration
description: sinnix-d72's overlay audit (2026-07-08) found flake/overlay/package/imv.nix's --enable-all/--with-backend=wayland configureFlags target an autotools build, but current nixpkgs imv builds with meson (withBackends list already defaults libjxl/libheif/libwebp on, no libavif backend) and no longer touches SDL2_image. The overlay's extra buildInputs (libavif, libheif, patched SDL2_image) look unreferenced by imv's current build. imvWithExtras is used in modules/features/desktop/media.nix:195. Needs an actual build/format-support test (not just source reading) to confirm the override is a no-op before dropping or rewriting it.
close_reason: Confirmed via real build evidence (not just source reading): built both imvWithExtras and plain imv, then (1) nix derivation show confirmed nixpkgs' current imv is meson-based with mesonFlags already enabling libheif/libjxl/libwebp and no libavif option at all -- the overlay's autotools-shaped configureFlags are declared but never consumed by meson's configure phase; (2) nix why-depends showed the built output does NOT depend on libavif or the patched SDL2_image despite them being listed as buildInputs; (3) ldd diff between the two binaries was byte-identical. The overlay was a confirmed no-op. Deleted flake/overlay/package/imv.nix (auto-discovered, no separate deregistration needed) and switched modules/features/desktop/media.nix from imvWithExtras to plain imv, updating the desktop-entry comment since no functionality was actually lost (upstream meson build already enables the relevant backends by default). Verified via nix eval/nix build against nixosConfigurations.sinnix-prime.

title: environmentAllowList implies an env boundary that scope mode does not enforce (and the list is stale)
description: sinnix-scope builds --setenv args from runtimeDefaults.environmentAllowList, but it launches via 'systemd-run --scope', where the command runs as a child of the caller and inherits the FULL environment — the allowlist filters nothing (verified by reading scripts/sinnix-scope:166-202 + systemd-run scope semantics). Consequences: (1) the list is dead weight that misleads readers into thinking an env boundary exists; (2) it is dangerously incomplete if anyone ever switches to service mode — it lacks ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN/ANTHROPIC_MODEL/DEEPSEEK_API_KEY/LITELLM_LOCAL_KEY/CONTEXT7_API_KEY/SINNIX_CLAUDE_PROFILE/SINNIX_CODEX_PROFILE, so claude-local/claude-deepseek would silently fall back to the real Anthropic backend and codex hook profile-gating would misfire. Fix: either delete the allowlist + --setenv plumbing and document scope-inheritance at the call site, or (if a real boundary is wanted) switch to service-mode launches and complete the list. Decide once; do not leave the phantom boundary.
acceptance_criteria: Either allowlist plumbing removed with an inheritance comment, or service-mode launch with a complete verified list (claude-local reaches LiteLLM through the scope)
close_reason: Deleted the phantom --setenv plumbing and environmentAllowList data table entirely (scripts/sinnix-scope, flake/data/runtime-defaults.nix) rather than build out a complete allowlist for a boundary systemd-run --scope can't enforce. Documented scope-inheritance explicitly in-script. Verified live: /etc/sinnix/runtime-inventory.json no longer has an environmentAllowList key.

title: Oracle digests are wiped every reboot — persist ~/.local/share/oracle
description: modules/services/oracle.nix writes daily digests to %h/.local/share/oracle (timer Persistent=true), but that path is not in sinnix.persistence.home.directories — the whole digest archive is destroyed on every boot (impermanence wipes @). Today's digest regenerates on the next timer fire but history is unrecoverable, and oracle v2 (sinnix-uha) wants an advice-fate feedback loop that NEEDS past digests. Fix: colocate a persistence declaration in oracle.nix per the repo convention (persistence declared next to the owning module). Sweep result for context: all other /var/lib and ~/.local state writers check out (open-webui persisted in-module, litellm documented stateless, sinnix-browser reseeds by design).
acceptance_criteria: ~/.local/share/oracle survives reboot; declaration lives in oracle.nix
close_reason: Added sinnix.persistence.home.directories = [ ".local/share/oracle" ]; inside oracle.nix's configFn (gated by cfg.enable like the rest of the module, per repo convention).

title: Remove unused llm-agents flake input
description: flake.nix declares llm-agents (github:numtide/llm-agents.nix, 'fast-moving agent CLI package supply') but the only reference in the tree is flake/test-lib.nix's sanitizedInputs enumeration — no package is ever consumed (verified by rg 2026-07-07; agent CLIs are npm-bootstrapped in agent-tools.nix instead). It is fetched and lock-bumped on every routine 'update' for nothing. Remove the input + the test-lib list entry in one pass (no-alias rule).
acceptance_criteria: Input gone from flake.nix/flake.lock and test-lib; update no longer fetches it; eval clean
close_reason: Removed the llm-agents flake input (flake.nix) and its sanitizedInputs re-export (flake/test-lib.nix) -- confirmed zero other references repo-wide before removal.

title: sshd.socket is a listen-less stub unit created only to set Slice
description: networking.nix:113 sets systemd.sockets.sshd.socketConfig.Slice, but services.openssh.startWhenNeeded is false so NixOS never defines a real sshd.socket — the config MATERIALIZES a stub unit with a [Socket] section containing only Slice= and no ListenStream (verified live: systemctl cat sshd.socket). Starting it would fail; runtime-defaults baseSurfaces also registers sshd-socket in the inventory as if it were a real surface. Fix: delete the systemd.sockets.sshd block and the sshd-socket surface entry (sshd.service placement already handles the interactive-access class), or deliberately adopt startWhenNeeded=true and keep both.
acceptance_criteria: No stub sshd.socket unit in the closure; inventory has no sshd-socket surface (or socket activation genuinely adopted); sshd.service placement unchanged
close_reason: Deleted the systemd.sockets.sshd stub block (networking.nix) and the sshd-socket baseSurfaces entry (runtime-defaults.nix). Verified live: systemctl cat sshd.socket now reports no unit.

title: Hyprland keybind & ergonomics pass
description: Grounded in bindings.nix audit 2026-07-07: (1) SUPER+G and SUPER+T BOTH bind togglegroup — reclaim G; add moveintogroup/moveoutofgroup binds (SUPER+[ / ] / SHIFT+[) so groups are buildable keyboard-only (group-tabs = 'browser tabs for terminals' on the 48" panel). (2) System submap (SUPER+End): screenshot modes, replay, park-slices, monitor submap (ASBL pulse/vibrance/HDR-shot) — keybind-cheatsheet plugin renders submaps as discoverable modes. (3) 'Summon agent here': SUPER+SHIFT+Return spawns claude/codex in the cwd of the focused kitty window (kitten @ ls). (4) OCR-to-clipboard bind: grim region -> tesseract/ocr service -> wl-copy. (5) cursor_zoom_factor magnifier binds + hyprpicker. (6) Smart fullscreen SUPER+SHIFT+F = fullscreen,1. (7) Dismiss-all-scratchpads bind. Ideation note F1-F7, G3.

title: Ambient intelligence cluster: circadian ring, fragmentation nudge, anomaly ticker, chores badge
description: Lynchpin-powered pattern-aware Noctalia surfaces (all read-only, rate-limited, mode-gated): (1) circadian position ring from measured hourly deep-work distribution (DAY_BOUNDARY_HOUR=6 aware); (2) fragmentation nudge when live context-switch rate exceeds personal p90 for 30min (one gentle toast, off by default); (3) daily anomaly ticker from lynchpin anomaly/changepoint machinery with click-through to evidence; (4) chores badge: sinnix dirty >24h, unpushed master commits, bd ready count, borg queue age — a personal ops inbox popout with jump actions. Optional/speculative: sleep-score tint (C7). Design rule: substrate queries cached daily via a small materialized status file, widgets never query DuckDB directly. Ideation note C2-C5.
acceptance_criteria: Ring reflects real circadian profile; nudge fires only above personal baseline and respects DND; ticker items link to evidence; chores badge counts verified against CLI truth

title: Agent fleet cockpit: live session panel, approval palette, outcome toasts, MCP meter
description: Noctalia panel for the agent fleet: (1) live sessions (claude/codex/gemini) with project, state (working / awaiting input / stuck via polylogue find_stuck_sessions + session_work_events), tokens this session, last-activity age; click focuses the owning kitty window.
