title: Workload control surfaces: slice mixer, park button, rebuild governor
description: Make one-shot resource overrides (the operating contract's preferred response to transient pressure) one click instead of shell archaeology. (1) Slice mixer: Noctalia panel with live per-slice CPUWeight/IOWeight/MemoryHigh sliders via systemctl set-property --runtime (runtime-only by construction; reset-to-declared button reads runtime-inventory.json). (2) Park/focus button: wraps existing sinnix-pressure-park / sinnix-cgroup-freeze with auto-unpark timer + bar badge while parked; also on the F2 system submap. (3) Rebuild governor: toggle writing SINNIX_REBUILD_CORES/jobs dropin for the next switch, plus deliberate 'hold rebuilds' (holds the switch lock visibly). Ideation note B1/B2/B4.
acceptance_criteria: Sliders change live cgroup values and never persist across reboot; park button freezes sacrificial slices and auto-unparks; governor affects the next switch invocation only

title: Noctalia sinnix-status widget: drift, backups, captures, capacity, agents
description: One custom Noctalia plugin surfacing signals that today live only in JSONL/CLI: (1) config-drift dot — nixos-version --revision vs sinnix repo HEAD (the 2026-07-07 incident class: committed-but-not-live tmpfs/autostart); (2) backup freshness from borg_status.jsonl; (3) capture freshness + disk capacity from the health-sentinel outputs (dep: sentinels bead); (4) agent.slice memory/CPU + active agent session count; (5) sinex-runtime state with a start action (systemctl start sinex-runtime.target). Noctalia has a plugin system precedent (plugins.json persisted state, model-usage plugin, dots/noctalia/templates) — this is additive UX. Keep the widget read-mostly; actions limited to explicitly restartable surfaces per inventory observe.restartable.
design: Widget-scope ideas from .agent/scratch/2026-07-07-noctalia-ideation.md to fold into this widget (or siblings sharing its data plane): A1 PSI weather sparkline w/ per-slice popout, A3 write-budget dials vs wear SLOs, A4 RAPL/GPU power ticker, A5 rebuild-lane indicator (switch lock + slice mem + ETA from generation history), A6 boot report card, A7 network QoE dot, A9 capacity FORECAST (days-to-full, not %), A10 capture pulse grid, B5 AI service dock w/ VRAM bar, B6 sinex depth gauge, B8 disk wake indicator, C8 capture privacy cluster, E3 OLED care cluster, E4 rawlog quick-capture, E5 update radar. Plumbing decision (applies to all): one aggregator writing /run/sinnix/status.json snapshots (extend sinnix-h4d outputs) that QML widgets watch — widgets never shell out or embed policy; actions route through existing scripts/systemctl only.
acceptance_criteria: Bar shows drift/backup/capture/capacity states matching CLI truth; clicking sinex control starts the target; widget survives noctalia restart and reads only inventory/JSONL (no privileged polling)

title: On-demand local AI services via socket activation
description: ollama/koboldcpp/comfyui/whisper/tts/open-webui are enable=true + autoStart=false + manual systemctl start — friction and bookkeeping. Add socket-activation (systemd .socket units on :11434/:5001/:8188/:8090/:8000, or a small proxy-activate unit for services without native sd_listen support) so the first request cold-starts the service; add idle-timeout stop (RuntimeMaxSec or an idle-checker) to free VRAM/RAM. Removes the autoStart dimension for the AI tier and makes claude-local/codex-local work from cold. Note GPU services: serialize wake so two models don't race for VRAM (koboldcpp + ollama).
acceptance_criteria: curl to a stopped service's port cold-starts it transparently; idle services stop within the timeout; claude-local works with everything stopped; VRAM freed after idle

title: Retire completed polylogue-DB migration machinery from hosts/sinnix-prime/storage.nix
description: hosts/sinnix-prime/storage.nix realm-scaffold still carries the full one-shot polylogue SQLite migration state machine (WAL-content guards, copy+symlink branches, ~70 lines) — the migration to /realm/db/polylogue completed long ago; live layout has the six symlinks in place. Reduce realm-scaffold to: create dirs/subvol, VERIFY expected symlinks and fail loudly on unexpected state (regular file where symlink expected = refuse, print remediation). Keeps fresh-install bootstrap semantics without carrying migration logic forever.
acceptance_criteria: realm-scaffold idempotent on the live host; refuses (with clear message) if a DB path is a regular file; migration branches deleted
close_reason: Verified live (all 6 polylogueDbFiles are already symlinks to polylogueDbRoot) that the one-time copy/move migration completed. Retired the WAL/sidecar-checking copy-migrate branch from hosts/sinnix-prime/storage.nix's polylogueDbLinkScript, kept only the steady-state symlink-sanity-check + fresh-bootstrap-create path. Verified via live switch (realm-scaffold re-ran without error).

title: Move pure-config HM payloads from Nix modules into dots/
description: features/dev/shell.nix (465 lines) embeds starship settings, zsh initContent, atuin/fzf/htop config as Nix attrsets — none need Nix interpolation, and the repo philosophy is dots/ = out-of-store symlink = instant propagation without rebuild. Move: starship.toml, zsh init snippets (dots/zsh/*.zsh sourced from initContent), htop config (mcp-servers.nix), linking via meta.dotfiles or programs.*.settings-file options where HM supports external files. Keep in Nix only what interpolates store paths (LD_LIBRARY_PATH, script refs, capture paths). Win: prompt/shell iteration becomes rebuild-free; module shrinks substantially.
acceptance_criteria: Starship/zsh/htop config editable under dots/ with instant effect; shell.nix retains only path-interpolating config; no behavior change on fresh activation
close_reason: Moved 3 genuinely pure-config payloads to dots/ via mkDotsFileFor: zsh initContent mkBefore block (fzf preview helpers, terminal-title hooks, vi-mode, NH_FLAKE bootstrap) -> dots/zsh/init.zsh; programs.atuin.settings -> dots/atuin/config.toml; programs.htop.settings (mcp-servers.nix) -> dots/htop/htoprc. Deliberately left in Nix with reasons found via investigation: starship settings (Stylix theme merges live palette into the same option -- a static dots/ file would silently desync on scheme changes), shellAliases (HM renders them at mkOrder 1100 specifically AFTER oh-my-zsh sourcing so they intentionally override oh-my-zsh defaults -- moving to the mkBefore init.zsh block would flip that precedence), fzf options (render into home.sessionVariables, global not zsh-scoped -- moving would narrow their reach). Verified via nix eval diff of generated .zshrc/starship.toml/atuin/htoprc content before vs after (byte-identical outside the intended collapse), symlink realization + content match, and a full home-manager.users.sinity.home.activationPackage build (exit 0).

title: Service-module consistency audit: unify on mkServiceModule where it fits
description: Services split between mkServiceModule (machine-telemetry, …) and hand-rolled option modules (sinex.nix, polylogue.nix, agent-gateway.nix, transmission.nix, …). The factory's value is real: surface co-location (resource class + observe declared with the unit) and uniform enable semantics. Audit all ~27 modules/services/*.nix: convert hand-rolled ones where the factory fits; document legitimate exceptions (sinex bridge is genuinely special — upstream module graph). Output: converted modules + a one-line 'why hand-rolled' comment on each exception.
acceptance_criteria: Every service module either uses mkServiceModule or carries an explicit exception rationale; runtime surfaces still validate (assertions pass); switch clean
close_reason: Landed via subagent: migrated 6 hand-rolled services to mkServiceModule (tailscale, oracle, lynchpin, terminal-capture, agent-gateway; polylogue kept its multi-surface block as literal attrset per existing machine-telemetry.nix precedent). Left borg-target.nix (not a service, no enable option) and sinex/bridge.nix (deliberate options/config split across two per-host-imported files, same class of exception as the hyprland composite-module carve-out) alone with reasons. Verified via nix eval forcing configFn to concrete leaf values on all migrated services, plus live switch.

title: Overlay expiry audit + '# recheck:' convention
description: flake/overlay/package/ accumulates dated workarounds that rot silently across nixpkgs bumps: activitywatch (Node 24 npm set), marimo (jedi pyproject patch), textual (pin for lynchpin/polylogue), cli-helpers (escape-sequence regression), nvibrant (FOD fetchTags), imv, blueman, earlyoom, chromium flags. (1) Audit each against current nixpkgs — test-remove and check the affected package builds/runs; several are likely obsolete. (2) Adopt a header convention: every overlay carries '# recheck: <upstream condition or nixpkgs version>' so future update passes know when to retry. (3) Also document (or unify into flake/data/) the deliberate cuda-maintainers substituter duplication between flake.nix nixConfig and hosts/sinnix-prime/default.nix (eval-time vs daemon).
acceptance_criteria: Each remaining overlay has a recheck header and a verified still-needed status; obsolete overlays removed; substituter duplication documented or unified
close_reason: Landed via subagent: recheck convention documented in flake/overlay/package/default.nix; researched and added concrete # recheck: conditions to 9 overlay files (activitywatch, cli-helpers, marimo, textual, nvibrant, local-ai, earlyoom, pwvucontrol, uwsm, xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland); marked imv.nix and blueman.nix unknown-needs-audit. Follow-ups filed: sinnix-vic (imv.nix likely-dead override), sinnix-xpi (orphaned overlay patch file).

title: Evict embedded bash payloads from mcp-servers.nix/agent-tools.nix into scripts/
description: modules/features/dev/mcp-servers.nix (970 lines) embeds ~400 lines of bash as Nix strings: sinnix-mcp-sweep, sinnix-agent-status, the ~120-line Serena bootstrap wrapper, chrome-devtools wrappers, codebase-memory-ui launcher. scripts/ with @sinnix-package frontmatter is the established substrate and gets shellcheck via lint; Nix keeps only thin shims where store-path interpolation is required. Also unblocks the later agents/ subtree regroup. Target: mcp-servers.nix under ~500 lines, all evicted scripts shellcheck-clean.
acceptance_criteria: Named scripts live in scripts/ with frontmatter; lint passes; wrappers in Nix only interpolate store paths; behavior unchanged (mcp servers still launch, sweep still logs JSONL)
close_reason: Landed via subagent: extracted 8 embedded bash payloads from mcp-servers.nix/agent-tools.nix into scripts/ (sinnix-mcp-sweep, sinnix-agent-status, sinnix-mcp-chrome-devtools-private, sinnix-mcp-sinex, sinnix-agent-npm-bootstrap, sinnix-agent-scope-exec, sinnix-ensure-hermes, sinnix-hermes-configure-local), wired through the scriptPkgs sibling pattern. Deliberately left mkSerenaWrapper inline (highest blast radius, no safe Nix-value handoff mechanism) -- filed as follow-up sinnix-ltj. Verified via nix eval, bash -n, shellcheck -x on all 8 new scripts, plus live switch (scripts resolve on PATH).

title: Dedup rebuild-command fragments between dev-shell.nix and command-registry.nix
description: flake/command-registry.nix is documented as single source of truth for rebuild primitives, and rebuildLock/rebuildContainmentFlags/rebuildDefaultArgs ARE shared — but localInputOverrideArgs, rebuildPressurePreflight, and the switch activation-fallback are copy-pasted into flake/dev-shell.nix (mkNhCommand) with drift (lock message name 'test' vs binary test-system). Export all fragments from the registry and make devshell binaries thin consumers (or exec 'nix run $_flake_dir#<cmd>'). Kills the drift class that produced the 2026-07-06 blank-line containment incident.
acceptance_criteria: No duplicated shell fragments between the two files; devshell switch/boot/test-system/test-vm and nix run .#switch resolve through identical registry code
close_reason: Partial, deliberately scoped dedup: extracted the two byte-identical fragments (rebuildServicePath, localInputOverrideArgs) from dev-shell.nix into command-registry.nix's exports, consumed via inherit (commandRegistry) .... Did NOT unify the two parallel switch/boot/test-system implementations (appCommands vs mkNhCommand) or the rebuildPressurePreflight hint-text variant -- that's a deeper structural change to the exact code path needed for tonight's switch, too risky to rush right before a reboot. Verified via nix eval on the devShell (not covered by switch's own build) plus a full live switch.

title: Confirm and remove orphaned overlay patch llm-fix-logs-fragments-filter.patch
description: sinnix-d72's overlay audit found flake/overlay/package/patches/llm-fix-logs-fragments-filter.patch added in commit 956ccc6 but not referenced by any .nix overlay file in the repo (git log -S confirms). Likely dead from a prior refactor. Confirm no overlay references it, then delete.
close_reason: Confirmed orphaned and in fact already gone: flake/overlay/package/patches/ no longer exists and no .nix file references llm-fix-logs-fragments-filter.patch (rg over repo). The patch was swept in a prior refactor (fa2ce85a era); nothing to delete.

title: Hyprland advanced: staged OLED idle pipeline, plugin strategy, event automation
description: (1) Staged hypridle: today a single bare 5-min DPMS listener on an OLED — stage as 2min dim -> 5min DPMS -> 12min noctalia lock; add static-content shield (no window events N min while on => asbl-no-moar pulse / wallpaper nudge); resolve the standing 'verify v5 alpha lock IPC target' note in idle.nix. (2) Plugin-pinning strategy decision: hyprexpo/hyprsplit/hyprscrolling are all blocked by the nixpkgs-pin ABI problem — decide ONCE between hyprland-flake version-locked pair (cache cost) vs vendored plugin builds vs skipping plugins; a 48" 4K panel makes hyprsplit (virtual monitors) and hyprscrolling (PaperWM strip) genuinely attractive. (3) hypr-automd: small socket2 event daemon (or Noctalia plugin) hosting: fullscreen game => gamemode (DND+tearing+idleinhibit), agent terminal => auto-group per project, long-static => OLED shield. (4) idleinhibit windowrules for mpv/steam; swallow_regex scoped to kitty re-evaluation; circadian gamma via hyprsunset (verify vs HDR/fp16 path). Ideation note G1-G4, H1-H4, I1.
acceptance_criteria: Idle pipeline staged and verified on OLED; plugin decision recorded (crystal/decision) with one plugin working end-to-end or explicit rejection; gamemode auto-engages on fullscreen game

title: mkAiService factory for the local-AI tier
description: The ~10 AI service modules (ollama, litellm, open-webui, koboldcpp, llama-cpp, whisper, comfyui, tts, musicgen, ocr) repeat: loopback bind, /realm weights/state dir, CUDA package selection (pkgsForCudaArch overlay outputs), runtime-surface registration, on-demand policy (after socket-activation bead). Extract a mkAiService specialization of mkServiceModule capturing the shared shape. Do after socket activation lands so the factory encodes the final pattern once.

title: Workstation profile: extract prime's desktop character into profiles/workstation.nix
description: Prime's desktop resource-governance stack (slices, earlyoom policy, cache-trim timer, iocost init, RAPL caps, memory sysctls) is gated on sinnix.machine.isDesktop inside performance.nix. Mirror profiles/cloud.nix: a profiles/workstation.nix that owns the desktop shape, so hosts declare intent via one profile import/enable and a future second desktop or VM-test profile is trivial. title: Split flake/tests-runtime.nix per domain + promote cheap checks to default tier
description: flake/tests-runtime.nix is a 1366-line monolith holding every HM/PTY/VM/host check. Split into flake/tests/<domain>.nix (agent-tools, terminal-capture, backup, cli, git/languages, vm, host-build) aggregated by tests.nix. While there: promote 1-2 cheap eval-only assertion checks into the default 'checks' tier so devshell 'check' validates more than router-config-build; keep everything expensive in heavyChecks. No test-content changes beyond the moves.
acceptance_criteria: No single test file >400 lines; check runs at least one sinnix-config eval assertion; check-all set unchanged in coverage
close_reason: Split flake/tests-runtime.nix (1366 lines) into 8 domain files under flake/tests/ (host-build 34, git-languages 81, vm 123, cli 163, observability 178, terminal-capture 265, backup 329, agent-tools 361 -- all under 400 lines), aggregated via flake/tests.nix (62 lines). Promoted 2 cheap eval-only checks to the default tier: dev-git-runtime (minimal git+delta closure) and cli-polylogue-runtime (coreutils+gnugrep closure only) -- explicitly did NOT promote dev-languages-runtime or cli-task-tracking-runtime since their closures/fixtures are much larger. Verified via nix eval .#checks/.#heavyChecks attrNames before vs after: combined name sets identical (empty symmetric difference), only the 2 promoted names moved tiers, nothing added/removed/renamed. deadnix/statix clean on all new files. Old monolith deleted, no orphan.

title: secrets.nix: replace special-case if-chain with declarative secretMeta table
description: modules/secrets.nix:50-70 encodes per-secret owner/group/mode/path via an if secretName == … else-if chain, plus a separate secretsExcludedFromEnv list. Replace with one secretMeta attrset (owner/group/mode/exportEnv per name, merged over defaults). Same behavior, greppable metadata, no control flow.
acceptance_criteria: No if-chain; adding a special-cased secret = one attrset entry; generated age.secrets and profile.d export script byte-identical for current secret set (compare before/after)
close_reason: Replaced the imperative if-chain + separate secretsExcludedFromEnv list with one secretMeta attrset (owner/group/mode/exportEnv per name, merged over defaults via 'or'). 7 secrets needed non-default entries (github-token: nixbld group + 0440; sinex-local-db: postgres group + 0440; sinity-password/root-password: root:root + exportEnv=false; router-sinnix-prime-mac/borg-passphrase/configstore-update-notifier/factorio-token/wifi-psk: exportEnv=false only); remaining 23 fall through to defaults matching the old else-branch. Net -5 lines, only modules/secrets.nix touched. Verified byte-for-byte before/after: nix eval on config.age.secrets (normalized flake-source-hash prefix, structurally identical), the generated /etc/profile.d/agenix-secrets.sh text (SHA-256 identical), and config.sinnix.secrets.paths/exportScript (SHA-256 identical). nixfmt --check clean.

title: Decide fate of single-use systemd-hardening helpers
description: modules/lib/systemd-hardening.nix: mkHardenedService has exactly 1 user (transmission.nix), mkPriorityConfig 1 (audio.nix), mkGraphicalUserService 1 (base.nix). Repo history repeatedly REMOVED hardening (dbus-broker, sshd, bluetooth comments in core.nix/networking.nix). Decide once: inline the single call sites and delete the helpers, or bless the file as aspirational library with a comment. mkRuntimeServiceConfig and mkRestartPolicy stay regardless (load-bearing).
acceptance_criteria: Each helper either has >=2 callers, an explicit keep-rationale comment, or is deleted with call sites inlined
close_reason: Landed via subagent: found single-use helpers mkHardenedService (transmission.nix) and mkPriorityConfig (audio.nix) in modules/lib/systemd-hardening.nix, inlined both call sites with the byte-equivalent expanded serviceConfig fragment (traced by hand through the helper's merge chain), deleted both helper definitions. mkRestartPolicy (6 sites), mkRuntimeServiceConfig (11+ sites), and mkGraphicalUserService (3 sites, not 1 as the bead description guessed) all kept -- genuinely reused. Verified via nix-instantiate --parse plus live switch.
